351.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'We were sitting with the Prophet ﷺ when he said: "I do not know how long I will remain among you, so follow the two who come after me" — and he pointed to Abu Bakr and Umar — "and hold firmly to the covenant of Ammar, and whatever Ibn Masud tells you, believe him."'
351.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah ibn al-Yaman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Kami sedang duduk bersama Nabi ﷺ ketika beliau bersabda: "Aku tidak tahu berapa lama aku akan tinggal di antara kalian, maka ikutilah dua orang yang datang setelahku" — dan beliau menunjuk kepada Abu Bakr dan Umar — "dan berpeganglah erat dengan perjanjian Ammar, dan apa pun yang disampaikan Ibn Masud kepada kalian, percayailah."'

352.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ used to supplicate for a man, and his supplication would reach that man, his children, and his grandchildren.
352.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah ibn al-Yaman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ apabila berdoa untuk seseorang, maka doanya itu akan mengenai orang tersebut, anak-anaknya, dan cucu-cucunya.

353.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'In the time of the Prophet ﷺ, a man would utter a word and thereby become a hypocrite. Yet today I hear such words from one of you ten times in a single gathering.'
353.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah ibn al-Yaman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Di zaman Nabi ﷺ, seseorang mengucapkan satu kata lalu menjadi munafik karenanya. Namun hari ini aku mendengar kata-kata seperti itu dari salah seorang di antara kalian sepuluh kali dalam satu majelis.'

354.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'I know what is with the Dajjal better than the Dajjal himself. He has two rivers flowing with him: one appears to the eye as white water, and the other appears as blazing fire. If any one of you encounters him, let him go to the river that appears to be fire, close his eyes, lower his head, and drink from it, for it is cold water. And the Dajjal has one eye blinded.'
354.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah ibn al-Yaman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Aku lebih mengetahui apa yang ada pada Dajjal daripada Dajjal itu sendiri. Bersamanya ada dua sungai yang mengalir: salah satunya tampak di mata seperti air putih, dan yang lain tampak seperti api yang membara. Jika salah seorang di antara kalian menemuinya, hendaklah ia mendatangi sungai yang tampak seperti api, memejamkan matanya, menundukkan kepalanya, lalu meminumnya, karena sesungguhnya itu adalah air yang dingin. Dan Dajjal itu buta satu matanya.'

355.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman (may Allah be pleased with him) that he returned from Umar and reported: 'When we sat with him yesterday, he asked the Companions of Muhammad ﷺ which of them had heard the words of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ about the trials. They said they had heard. He said: "Perhaps you mean the trial of a man in his family and wealth?" They said: "Yes." He said: "I am not asking about that. Prayer, fasting, and charity expiate for those. But which of you heard the words of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ about the trials that surge like the waves of the sea?"'
355.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah ibn al-Yaman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia kembali dari Umar dan menceritakan: 'Ketika kami duduk bersamanya kemarin, dia bertanya kepada para Sahabat Muhammad ﷺ siapa di antara mereka yang mendengar sabda Rasulullah ﷺ tentang fitnah-fitnah. Mereka berkata bahwa mereka telah mendengarnya. Dia berkata: "Mungkin maksud kalian fitnah seseorang dalam keluarga dan hartanya?" Mereka berkata: "Ya." Dia berkata: "Bukan itu yang aku tanyakan. Shalat, puasa, dan sedekah menghapus fitnah-fitnah itu. Namun siapa di antara kalian yang mendengar sabda Rasulullah ﷺ tentang fitnah-fitnah yang bergelombang seperti ombak lautan?"'

356.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ informed me of everything that will happen until the Hour is established. There is nothing of it that I did not ask him about, except that I did not ask him what will cause the people of Madinah to leave Madinah.'
356.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah ibn al-Yaman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ memberitahuku tentang segala sesuatu yang akan terjadi hingga hari Kiamat. Tidak ada satu pun yang tidak aku tanyakan kepadanya, kecuali aku tidak menanyakan apa yang akan membuat penduduk Madinah meninggalkan Madinah.'

357.iwh_en: It was narrated that Nasr ibn Asim al-Laythi said: 'We came to al-Yashkuri with a group from Banu Layth. He asked who we were and we said: "Banu Layth." We asked him about the hadith of Hudhayfa. He said: "We were returning with Abu Musa and the riding animals had become expensive in Kufa, so I and a companion asked Abu Musa for permission to go ahead, and he gave us permission." He then described meeting Hudhayfa and hearing him say something about the trials and straying from the community.'
357.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan bahwa Nasr ibn Ashim al-Laitsi berkata: 'Kami mendatangi al-Yashkuri bersama sekelompok orang dari Banu Laits. Dia bertanya siapa kami dan kami berkata: "Banu Laits." Kami bertanya kepadanya tentang hadits Hudzaifah. Dia berkata: "Kami sedang kembali bersama Abu Musa dan harga tunggangan naik di Kufah, maka aku dan seorang temanku meminta izin kepada Abu Musa untuk berangkat duluan, dan dia mengizinkan kami." Kemudian dia menceritakan pertemuannya dengan Hudzaifah dan mendengarnya berbicara tentang fitnah-fitnah dan penyimpangan dari jamaah.'

358.iwh_en: It was narrated that Rib'i ibn Hirash said: 'I went to Hudhayfa in al-Mada'in during the days when people were marching against Uthman. He said: "O Rib'i, what are your people doing?" I said: "Which aspect of their affairs do you ask about?" He said: "Who among them has gone out against this man?" I named several men who had gone. Then Hudhayfa said: "I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: 'Whoever parts from the community and scorns authority will meet Allah Almighty with no standing before Him."'
358.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan bahwa Rib'i ibn Hirasy berkata: 'Aku pergi menemui Hudzaifah di al-Mada'in pada hari-hari ketika orang-orang berbaris menuju Utsman. Dia berkata: "Wahai Rib'i, apa yang dilakukan kaummu?" Aku berkata: "Aspek mana dari keadaan mereka yang engkau tanyakan?" Dia berkata: "Siapa di antara mereka yang keluar melawan orang ini?" Aku menyebutkan beberapa nama yang telah pergi. Kemudian Hudzaifah berkata: "Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa memisahkan diri dari jamaah dan meremehkan kepemimpinan, dia akan bertemu Allah Azza wa Jalla tanpa memiliki kedudukan di sisi-Nya."'

359.iwh_en: It was narrated that Zirr ibn Hubaysh said: 'I came upon Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman while he was narrating about the night the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ was taken on the Night Journey. He was saying: "Then we set out" or "I set out" until we reached Jerusalem, but they did not enter it.' Zirr said: 'I said: "But the Messenger of Allah ﷺ did enter it that night and prayed therein." Hudhayfa asked: "What is your name, O bald one? I recognize your face but do not know your name." I said: "I am Zirr ibn Hubaysh." He said: "And how do you know that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ prayed therein?"'
359.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan bahwa Zirr ibn Hubaysh berkata: 'Aku mendatangi Hudzaifah ibn al-Yaman sementara dia sedang menceritakan tentang malam Nabi Muhammad ﷺ diisra'kan. Dia berkata: "Kemudian kami berangkat" atau "aku berangkat" hingga kami tiba di Baitul Maqdis, namun mereka berdua tidak memasukinya.' Zirr berkata: 'Aku berkata: "Namun Rasulullah ﷺ memang memasukinya malam itu dan shalat di dalamnya." Hudzaifah bertanya: "Siapa namamu, wahai orang yang botak? Aku mengenali wajahmu namun tidak tahu namamu." Aku berkata: "Aku Zirr ibn Hubaysh." Dia berkata: "Dan dari mana kamu tahu bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ shalat di dalamnya?"'

360.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ used to place his right hand under his right cheek when he lay down to sleep at night, then say: 'O Allah, by Your Name I live and by Your Name I die.' And when he awoke during the night he would say: 'All praise is due to Allah who has given me life after causing me to die, and to Him is the resurrection.'
360.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah ibn al-Yaman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ biasa meletakkan tangan kanannya di bawah pipi kanannya ketika berbaring tidur di malam hari, kemudian berdoa: 'Ya Allah, dengan nama-Mu aku hidup dan dengan nama-Mu aku mati.' Dan ketika terbangun di malam hari beliau berdoa: 'Segala puji bagi Allah yang telah menghidupkanku setelah mematikanku, dan kepada-Nya lah kebangkitan.'

361.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The virtue of the house close to the mosque over the house far from it is like the virtue of the fighter in Allah's cause over the one who stays behind.'
361.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah ibn al-Yaman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Keutamaan rumah yang dekat dengan masjid atas rumah yang jauh darinya adalah seperti keutamaan orang yang berperang di jalan Allah atas orang yang tidak berangkat.'

362.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman (may Allah be pleased with him) through Rib'i ibn Hirash that Rib'i visited him in al-Mada'in. Hudhayfa asked: 'O Rib'i, have any of your people gone out?' He said: 'Yes,' and named some men — this was at the time when people were marching against Uthman. Hudhayfa said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "Whoever parts from the community and scorns the authority will meet Allah with no standing before Him."'
362.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah ibn al-Yaman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melalui Rib'i ibn Hirasy bahwa Rib'i mengunjunginya di al-Mada'in. Hudzaifah bertanya: 'Wahai Rib'i, apakah ada dari kaummu yang keluar?' Dia berkata: 'Ya,' dan menyebutkan beberapa orang — ini terjadi pada saat orang-orang berbaris melawan Utsman. Hudzaifah berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Barangsiapa memisahkan diri dari jamaah dan meremehkan kepemimpinan, dia akan bertemu Allah tanpa memiliki kedudukan di sisi-Nya."'

363.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'A man begged in the time of the Prophet ﷺ, and the people held back. Then one man gave to him, and then the people gave. The Prophet ﷺ said: "Whoever sets a good precedent that is followed shall have its reward and a share of the reward of all who follow it, without their rewards being diminished. And whoever sets a bad precedent that is followed shall bear its burden and a share of the burden of all who follow it, without their burdens being diminished."'
363.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah ibn al-Yaman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Seorang laki-laki meminta-minta di zaman Nabi ﷺ, dan orang-orang menahan diri. Kemudian seorang laki-laki memberinya, lalu orang-orang pun memberi. Nabi ﷺ bersabda: "Barangsiapa membuat sunnah yang baik lalu diikuti, maka dia mendapat pahalanya dan sebagian dari pahala semua yang mengikutinya tanpa mengurangi pahala mereka sedikit pun. Dan barangsiapa membuat sunnah yang buruk lalu diikuti, maka dia menanggung dosanya dan sebagian dari dosa semua yang mengikutinya tanpa mengurangi dosa mereka sedikit pun."'

364.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Certain groups will come to my Fountain and be driven away from me. I will say: "O Lord, my Companions! O Lord, my Companions!" Then it will be said to me: "You do not know what they introduced after you."'
364.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Beberapa kelompok akan datang ke telagaku kemudian diusir dariku. Aku akan berkata: "Ya Rabb, para Sahabatku! Ya Rabb, para Sahabatku!" Kemudian akan dikatakan kepadaku: "Engkau tidak tahu apa yang mereka ada-adakan setelahmu."'

365.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'By Allah, I am the most knowledgeable of people about every trial that will occur between me and the Hour. That is not because the Messenger of Allah ﷺ confided something to me that he did not confide to others. Rather, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ spoke while I was in a gathering, and he was asked about the trials, so he told us about them.'
365.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah ibn al-Yaman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Demi Allah, aku adalah orang yang paling mengetahui setiap fitnah yang akan terjadi antara aku dan hari Kiamat. Itu bukan karena Rasulullah ﷺ mempercayakan sesuatu kepadaku yang tidak beliau sampaikan kepada orang lain. Akan tetapi, Rasulullah ﷺ berbicara sementara aku berada dalam suatu majelis, dan beliau ditanya tentang fitnah-fitnah, lalu beliau memberitahukan kepada kami.'

366.iwh_en: It was narrated from a freed slave of Shurahbil ibn Hasana that he heard Uqba ibn Amir al-Juhani and Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman both say: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Eat whatever your bow returns to you."'
366.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari bekas budak Shurahbil ibn Hasanah bahwa dia mendengar Uqbah ibn Amir al-Juhani dan Hudzaifah ibn al-Yaman keduanya berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Makanlah apa pun yang dikembalikan busurmu kepadamu."'

367.iwh_en: It was narrated from a freed slave of Shurahbil ibn Hasana that he heard Uqba ibn Amir al-Juhani and Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman both say: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Eat whatever your bow returns to you."'
367.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari bekas budak Shurahbil ibn Hasanah bahwa dia mendengar Uqbah ibn Amir al-Juhani dan Hudzaifah ibn al-Yaman keduanya berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Makanlah apa pun yang dikembalikan busurmu kepadamu."'

368.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The master of the children of Adam on the Day of Resurrection is Muhammad ﷺ.'
368.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Pemimpin anak Adam pada hari Kiamat adalah Muhammad ﷺ.'

369.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The master of the children of Adam on the Day of Resurrection is Muhammad ﷺ.'
369.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Pemimpin anak Adam pada hari Kiamat adalah Muhammad ﷺ.'

370.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The master of the children of Adam on the Day of Resurrection is Muhammad ﷺ.'
370.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Pemimpin anak Adam pada hari Kiamat adalah Muhammad ﷺ.'

371.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The master of the children of Adam on the Day of Resurrection is Muhammad ﷺ.'
371.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Pemimpin anak Adam pada hari Kiamat adalah Muhammad ﷺ.'

372.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman (may Allah be pleased with him) through his brother Abd al-Aziz that he said: 'When the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was distressed by a matter, he would pray.'
372.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah ibn al-Yaman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melalui saudaranya Abd al-Aziz bahwa dia berkata: 'Apabila Rasulullah ﷺ menghadapi suatu urusan yang berat, beliau melakukan shalat.'

373.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I stood with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ one night and he recited the seven long surahs in seven rak'ahs. When he raised his head from bowing he would say: "Allah hears whoever praises Him," then he would say: "All praise is due to Allah, the Lord of dominion, might, majesty, and greatness." His bowing was equal in length to his standing, and his prostration was equal in length to his bowing. He finished and my legs had nearly broken.'
373.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah ibn al-Yaman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Aku berdiri bersama Rasulullah ﷺ suatu malam dan beliau membaca tujuh surah panjang dalam tujuh rakaat. Ketika beliau mengangkat kepalanya dari ruku, beliau mengucapkan: "Allah mendengar siapa yang memuji-Nya," kemudian beliau mengucapkan: "Segala puji bagi Allah, Pemilik kerajaan, keperkasaan, kebesaran, dan keagungan." Rukuknya sama panjangnya dengan berdirinya, dan sujudnya sama panjangnya dengan rukuknya. Selesai dan kedua kakiku hampir patah.'

374.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'By Him in Whose hand is my soul, you must enjoin what is right and forbid what is wrong, or Allah will be about to send upon you a punishment from Himself, and then you will supplicate to Him but He will not respond to you.'
374.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah ibn al-Yaman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Demi Dzat yang jiwaku berada di tangan-Nya, kalian harus memerintahkan yang baik dan melarang yang mungkar, atau Allah akan segera mengirimkan hukuman dari sisi-Nya kepada kalian, kemudian kalian berdoa kepada-Nya namun Dia tidak mengabulkan doa kalian.'

375.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'The Hour will not be established until you kill your leader, strike one another with your swords, and the worst of you inherit your dwellings.'
375.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah ibn al-Yaman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Hari Kiamat tidak akan terjadi hingga kalian membunuh pemimpin kalian, saling menyerang dengan pedang kalian, dan orang-orang terburuk di antara kalian mewarisi tempat tinggal kalian.'

376.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'The Hour will not be established until the most prosperous of people in worldly terms is a vile man, son of a vile man.'
376.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah ibn al-Yaman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Hari Kiamat tidak akan terjadi hingga orang yang paling beruntung di dunia adalah orang yang hina, anak dari orang yang hina.'

377.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Dajjal was mentioned in the presence of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and he said: 'The trial of some of you among yourselves is more fearful to me than the trial of the Dajjal. None will escape what came before the Dajjal's trial except one who also escapes his trial. No trial, large or small, has ever arisen since the world began, except that it will be as nothing compared to the trial of the Dajjal.'
377.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Dajjal disebutkan di hadapan Rasulullah ﷺ dan beliau bersabda: 'Fitnah sebagian dari kalian terhadap sesama kalian lebih aku khawatirkan daripada fitnah Dajjal. Tidak ada yang akan selamat dari apa yang terjadi sebelum fitnah Dajjal kecuali yang juga selamat darinya. Tidak ada fitnah, besar maupun kecil, yang pernah muncul sejak dunia ada, kecuali bahwa ia tidak ada apa-apanya dibanding fitnah Dajjal.'

378.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man passed by him and it was said: 'This man reports news to the rulers.' Hudhayfa said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say — or: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said — "A tale-bearer will not enter Paradise."'
378.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki melewatinya dan dikatakan: 'Laki-laki ini melaporkan berita kepada para penguasa.' Hudzaifah berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda — atau: Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda — "Penyebar fitnah tidak akan masuk surga."'

379.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was asked about the Hour and said: 'Its knowledge is with my Lord. None but He reveals it at its appointed time. But I will inform you of its signs and what precedes it. Before it there will be trials and harj.' They asked: 'O Messenger of Allah, the trial we know, but what is harj?' He said: 'In the Abyssinian language it means killing. And estrangement will be cast among people such that a man will barely recognize another.'
379.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ ditanya tentang hari Kiamat dan beliau bersabda: 'Ilmunya ada pada Tuhanku. Tidak ada yang menyingkapnya pada waktunya selain Dia. Namun aku akan memberitahu kalian tentang tanda-tandanya dan apa yang mendahuluinya. Sebelumnya akan ada fitnah dan harj.' Mereka bertanya: 'Ya Rasulullah, fitnah sudah kami ketahui, lalu apa itu harj?' Beliau bersabda: 'Dalam bahasa Habasyah artinya pembunuhan. Dan keterasingan akan ditimpakan di antara manusia sehingga seseorang hampir tidak bisa mengenal orang lain.'

380.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man at his funeral said he had heard Hudhayfa say: 'I have nothing to fear regarding what I heard from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. And if you fight one another, I will enter my house. If someone enters upon me, I will say: "Take upon yourself my sin and your own sin."'
380.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah ibn al-Yaman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki saat pemakamannya berkata bahwa dia mendengar Hudzaifah berkata: 'Aku tidak khawatir tentang apa yang aku dengar dari Rasulullah ﷺ. Dan jika kalian saling berperang, aku akan masuk ke rumahku. Jika seseorang memasukinya, aku akan berkata: "Tanggungkanlah dosaku dan dosamu sendiri."'

381.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman (may Allah be pleased with him) that Abd al-Rahman ibn Yazid said: 'We came to Hudhayfa and said: "Guide us to the person closest to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ in conduct, bearing, and loyalty, so that we may take from him and learn from him." He said: "The closest of people to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ in conduct, bearing, and demeanor is Ibn Umm Abd, even when he goes into his house out of my sight. And the noble Companions of Muhammad ﷺ all know that Ibn Umm Abd is among the closest of them."'
381.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah ibn al-Yaman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Abd al-Rahman ibn Yazid berkata: 'Kami mendatangi Hudzaifah dan berkata: "Tunjukkan kepada kami orang yang paling dekat dengan Rasulullah ﷺ dalam perilaku, penampilan, dan kesetiaan, agar kami bisa mengambil ilmu darinya dan mendengar darinya." Dia berkata: "Orang yang paling dekat dengan Rasulullah ﷺ dalam perilaku, penampilan, dan sikap adalah Ibn Umm Abd, bahkan saat dia masuk ke rumahnya dan tidak terlihat olehku. Dan para Sahabat Muhammad ﷺ yang terpilih semuanya tahu bahwa Ibn Umm Abd termasuk yang paling dekat di antara mereka."'

382.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ stood among us and gave a speech in which he left out nothing that would happen before the Hour. Whoever memorized it memorized it, and whoever forgot it forgot it. I see a thing and I recall it just as a man recognizes the face of someone who was absent from him and then he sees him and recognizes him.'
382.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ berdiri di antara kami dan berkhutbah di mana beliau tidak meninggalkan satu pun hal yang akan terjadi sebelum hari Kiamat. Siapa yang menghafalnya menghafalnya, dan siapa yang melupakannya melupakannya. Aku melihat sesuatu dan aku mengingatnya seperti seseorang yang mengenali wajah orang yang lama tidak terlihat olehnya kemudian melihatnya kembali dan mengenalinya.'

383.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man used to carry reports about Hudhayfa to Uthman. Hudhayfa said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "A tale-bearer — meaning a gossip-monger — will not enter Paradise."'
383.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki biasa membawa laporan tentang Hudzaifah kepada Utsman. Hudzaifah berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Penyebar fitnah — yaitu orang yang gemar mengadu domba — tidak akan masuk surga."'

384.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ, when passing a verse of fear during recitation, would seek refuge in Allah, and when passing a verse of mercy, he would ask. And when the Prophet ﷺ bowed, he would say: 'Glory be to my Lord, the Mighty,' and when he prostrated, he would say: 'Glory be to my Lord, the Most High.'
384.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ apabila melewati ayat yang mengandung ancaman saat membaca, beliau berlindung kepada Allah, dan apabila melewati ayat rahmat, beliau memohon. Dan apabila Nabi ﷺ ruku, beliau mengucapkan: 'Subhana Rabbiya al-Adzim (Maha Suci Tuhanku Yang Maha Agung),' dan apabila bersujud, beliau mengucapkan: 'Subhana Rabbiya al-A'la (Maha Suci Tuhanku Yang Maha Tinggi).'

385.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Ruqad that he said: 'I went out with my master when I was a young boy and was brought before Hudhayfa, who was saying: "In the time of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, a man would say a word and thereby become a hypocrite. Yet I hear such words from one of you four times in a single sitting. You must enjoin what is right, forbid what is wrong, and urge one another to good — otherwise Allah will afflict you all with punishment, or He will cause the worst of you to rule over you and then the righteous among you will supplicate but their supplication will not be answered."'
385.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Ruqad bahwa dia berkata: 'Aku pergi bersama tuanku ketika aku masih kecil dan dibawa ke hadapan Hudzaifah, yang sedang berkata: "Di zaman Rasulullah ﷺ, seseorang mengucapkan satu kata lalu menjadi munafik karenanya. Namun aku mendengar kata-kata seperti itu dari salah seorang di antara kalian empat kali dalam satu dudukan. Kalian harus memerintahkan yang baik, melarang yang mungkar, dan saling mendorong untuk kebaikan — jika tidak, Allah akan menimpa kalian semua dengan azab, atau Dia akan menjadikan orang-orang terburuk di antara kalian berkuasa atas kalian, kemudian orang-orang baik di antara kalian berdoa namun doa mereka tidak dikabulkan."'

386.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to clean his mouth with the toothstick when he rose for the night prayer.'
386.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ biasa membersihkan mulutnya dengan siwak ketika bangkit untuk shalat malam.'

387.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "Do not drink from vessels of gold or silver, and do not wear silk or brocade, for these are for them in this world and for you in the Hereafter."'
387.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Janganlah kalian minum dari bejana emas atau perak, dan janganlah kalian memakai sutra atau brocade, karena sesungguhnya itu untuk mereka (orang-orang kafir) di dunia dan untuk kalian di akhirat."'

388.iwh_en: It was narrated from Thabit ibn Wadi'a that a man from Banu Fazara came to the Prophet ﷺ with lizards he had hunted. He began turning one over in front of the Prophet ﷺ and the Prophet ﷺ said: 'A community that was transformed.' He then said: 'To the best of my knowledge he said: "I do not know what became of it" — and: "I do not know, perhaps this is from them."' Hudhayfa narrated something similar from his own transmission and added that the Prophet ﷺ neither commanded nor forbade eating them.
388.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Tsabit ibn Wadi'ah bahwa seorang laki-laki dari Banu Fazarah datang kepada Nabi ﷺ dengan membawa biawak yang telah dia buru. Dia mulai membolak-balik seekor biawak di hadapan Nabi ﷺ dan Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Suatu umat yang telah diubah bentuknya.' Kemudian beliau berkata: 'Sepengetahuanku beliau bersabda: "Aku tidak tahu apa yang terjadi padanya" — dan: "Aku tidak tahu, mungkin ini dari mereka."' Hudzaifah meriwayatkan hal serupa dari jalurnya sendiri dan menambahkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ tidak memerintahkan maupun melarang memakannya.

389.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "This tribe of Mudar will not leave on earth a righteous servant of Allah except that they corrupt him and destroy him, until Allah overtakes them with soldiers from His servants and humiliates them until they cannot protect even the tail of a valley stream."'
389.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Kabilah Mudhar ini tidak akan meninggalkan seorang hamba Allah yang saleh di muka bumi kecuali mereka akan merusak dan membinasakannya, hingga Allah menguasai mereka dengan pasukan dari hamba-hamba-Nya dan menghinakan mereka sehingga mereka tidak mampu melindungi bahkan ekor aliran lembah."'

390.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'My Fountain stretches as far as the distance between Ayla and Mudar. Its vessels are more numerous than — or like — the count of the stars of the sky. Its water is sweeter than honey, whiter than milk, colder than snow, and more fragrant than musk. Whoever drinks from it will never be thirsty again.' In another narration: 'The two ends of the Prophet's ﷺ Fountain are as far apart as Ayla...'
390.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Telagaku membentang sejauh jarak antara Ailah dan Mudhar. Bejananya lebih banyak dari — atau seperti — jumlah bintang-bintang di langit. Airnya lebih manis dari madu, lebih putih dari susu, lebih dingin dari salju, dan lebih harum dari misk. Barangsiapa minum darinya tidak akan pernah haus lagi.' Dalam riwayat lain: 'Dua ujung telaga Nabi ﷺ sejauh jarak antara Ailah...'

391.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ammar ibn Yasir (may Allah be pleased with him) through Qays who said: 'I asked Ammar: "Regarding what you did in the matter of Ali — was it an opinion you held, or something the Messenger of Allah ﷺ instructed you?" He said: "The Messenger of Allah ﷺ did not instruct us with anything that he did not instruct all people with. But Hudhayfa informed me from the Prophet ﷺ that he said: 'Among my Companions are twelve hypocrites, of whom eight will not enter Paradise...'"'
391.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ammar ibn Yasir (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melalui Qais yang berkata: 'Aku bertanya kepada Ammar: "Mengenai apa yang kalian lakukan dalam urusan Ali — apakah itu pendapat yang kalian pegang, atau sesuatu yang diperintahkan Rasulullah ﷺ kepada kalian?" Dia berkata: "Rasulullah ﷺ tidak memerintahkan kami dengan sesuatu yang tidak beliau perintahkan kepada semua orang. Tetapi Hudzaifah memberitahuku dari Nabi ﷺ bahwa beliau bersabda: 'Di antara para Sahabatku ada dua belas orang munafik, delapan di antaranya tidak akan masuk surga...'"'

392.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Prophet ﷺ did not pray in Jerusalem. Had he prayed therein, your Prophet's ﷺ prayer there would have been prescribed upon you.'
392.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Nabi ﷺ tidak shalat di Baitul Maqdis. Seandainya beliau shalat di sana, maka shalat Nabi kalian ﷺ di tempat itu akan diwajibkan atas kalian.'

393.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman (may Allah be pleased with him) through Abu al-Tufayl that there was a dispute between Hudhayfa and a man from the people of al-Aqaba. The man said: 'I implore you by Allah, how many were the people of al-Aqaba?' The people told Hudhayfa: 'Tell him since he asked.' Hudhayfa said: 'We were informed they were fourteen.' The man said: 'I was informed they were fourteen.' Hudhayfa said: 'And if you were one of them...'
393.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah ibn al-Yaman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melalui Abu al-Thufail bahwa ada perselisihan antara Hudzaifah dan seorang laki-laki dari penduduk al-Aqabah. Laki-laki itu berkata: 'Aku bersumpah kepadamu demi Allah, berapa banyak orang yang hadir di al-Aqabah?' Orang-orang berkata kepada Hudzaifah: 'Beritahulah dia karena dia bertanya.' Hudzaifah berkata: 'Kami diberitahu bahwa mereka berjumlah empat belas.' Laki-laki itu berkata: 'Aku diberitahu bahwa mereka berjumlah empat belas.' Hudzaifah berkata: 'Dan jika kamu adalah salah satu dari mereka...'

394.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'No tents after the tents that were with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ have ever been in a place from which evil was repelled more abundantly than those tents that were pitched in this spot.'
394.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Tidak ada tenda-tenda setelah tenda-tenda yang bersama Rasulullah ﷺ yang pernah berada di suatu tempat di mana keburukan dihindarkan lebih banyak daripada tenda-tenda yang didirikan di tempat ini.'

395.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'You, O Arabs, today commit matters that were considered open hypocrisy in the time of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.'
395.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Kalian, wahai bangsa Arab, hari ini melakukan perkara-perkara yang di zaman Rasulullah ﷺ merupakan kemunafikan yang nyata.'

396.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'A people will come out of the Fire after it has burned them. They will be called al-Jahannamiyyun (the people of Hell).'
396.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah ibn al-Yaman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Suatu kaum akan keluar dari neraka setelah api melalap mereka. Mereka akan disebut al-Jahannamiyyun (orang-orang neraka).'

397.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I was supporting the Prophet ﷺ against my chest when he said: "Whoever says La ilaha illallah" — seeking the face of Allah — "and this is sealed for him at the end of his life, he will enter Paradise. And whoever fasts a day seeking the face of Allah and this is sealed for him, he will enter Paradise. And whoever gives charity seeking the face of Allah and this is sealed for him, he will enter Paradise."'
397.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Aku sedang menyandarkan Nabi ﷺ ke dadaku ketika beliau bersabda: "Barangsiapa mengucapkan La ilaha illallah" — karena mencari ridha Allah — "dan ini menjadi akhir hidupnya, dia akan masuk surga. Dan barangsiapa berpuasa satu hari karena mencari ridha Allah dan ini menjadi penutup hidupnya, dia akan masuk surga. Dan barangsiapa bersedekah karena mencari ridha Allah dan ini menjadi penutup hidupnya, dia akan masuk surga."'

398.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he heard of a man spreading gossip. Hudhayfa said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "A gossip-monger will not enter Paradise."'
398.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia mendengar tentang seorang laki-laki yang menyebarkan gossip. Hudzaifah berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Orang yang suka mengadu domba tidak akan masuk surga."'

399.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "The Quran was revealed in seven modes (ahruf)."'
399.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Al-Quran diturunkan dalam tujuh huruf (ahruf)."'

400.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hudhayfa ibn al-Yaman (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'By Him in Whose hand is my soul, you must enjoin what is right and forbid what is wrong, or Allah will send upon you a people and then you will call upon Him but He will not respond to you.'
400.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah ibn al-Yaman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Demi Dzat yang jiwaku berada di tangan-Nya, kalian harus memerintahkan yang baik dan melarang yang mungkar, atau Allah akan mengirimkan kepada kalian suatu kaum kemudian kalian berdoa kepada-Nya namun Dia tidak mengabulkan doa kalian.'
